mnemonics: I Drink Deeply For Success - Iterative Deepening Depth First Search (IDDFS) combines the memory efficiency of DFS with the completeness of BFS by gradually increasing the depth limit.
keyPoints: It performs a series of depth-limited DFS searches, incrementally increasing the depth limit with each iteration.
It is complete (will find a solution if one exists) and optimal for unweighted graphs (finds the shallowest solution).
It avoids the memory explosion of BFS while maintaining its advantages, though nodes on the final depth are generated multiple times.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with standard DFS and forgetting that it involves multiple iterations of depth-limited search.
Assuming it is inefficient due to node regeneration, without recognizing its optimal space complexity O(bd), where b is branching factor and d is depth.
